platform: WordPress with server-rendered search, scene, and performer pages

metadata_mapping.scene_title: Scene page VideoObject JSON-LD `name`; fallback to HTML title
metadata_mapping.date: Scene page VideoObject JSON-LD `uploadDate`, converted to YYYY-MM-DD
metadata_mapping.description: Scene page VideoObject JSON-LD `description`; fallback to meta description
metadata_mapping.performers: Scene page VideoObject JSON-LD `actor[]`; fallback to `/performer/` links
metadata_mapping.tags: Scene page VideoObject JSON-LD `keywords`; fallback to `/categories/` links
metadata_mapping.performer_name: Performer page Person JSON-LD `name`; fallback to H1/title
metadata_mapping.performer_image: Performer page Person JSON-LD `image`; fallback to first scene thumbnail
metadata_mapping.performer_details: Performer page Person JSON-LD `description`; fallback to meta description

scene_json_ld: Find `<script type="application/ld+json">` blocks and parse the `VideoObject` payload
scene_title: `<title>` without the ` - Watch gay porn for free on GayForFans.com` suffix
description: VideoObject `description` is templated site copy and may need light cleanup
performers: Fallback: `/performer/` anchors on the scene page
search_results: Search result cards are `<article>` nodes whose class list includes `video`
Card title lives in `h3.post-title a` and URL lives on linked thumbnail/title anchors
performer_json_ld: Performer pages include a Person JSON-LD block with `name`, `description`, `url`, and `image`

search_strategy.notes: Use the `/?s={query}` WordPress-style search endpoint. Parse video result cards, dedupe by URL, then rank exact normalized title matches above fuzzy matches.

fragment_matching_strategy.notes: Derive a query from title/name/filename/path, normalize it by removing extensions, codec/resolution noise, and long numeric suffixes, then search and scrape the top sufficiently similar match.

anti_bot_or_access_notes: No Cloudflare or captcha challenge was observed during live testing on May 11, 2026.
A browser-like User-Agent and normal HTML request headers were sufficient.
Scene and performer metadata are available without JavaScript execution because the useful JSON-LD is server-rendered.
known_limitations: Scene descriptions are templated marketing blurbs rather than rich editorial synopses.
The site search endpoint can return many near-duplicate scenes for popular performer names.
Fragment matching depends on title similarity and may skip weak matches below the confidence threshold.
Performer pages do not expose rich structured biographical metadata beyond name, image, and description.
